questions.assertion: Assertion (A): International trade is the exchange of goods and services between countries.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a vital component of international business.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: International trade involves the exchange of goods and services between countries, and it is indeed a vital component of international business.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Export Oriented Units (EOUs) are set up to cater to the requirements of the domestic market.
questions.reason: Reason (R): They are allowed to import duty-free inputs and export the final products.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Export Oriented Units (EOUs) are set up to cater to the requirements of the international market, not the domestic market. They are allowed to import duty-free inputs and export the final products.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) is the transfer of funds from one country to another for business purposes.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It involves the acquisition of an existing enterprise in a foreign country.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) is the transfer of funds from one country to another for the purpose of establishing a business, not just for business purposes. It can involve the acquisition of an existing enterprise in a foreign country or the establishment of a new enterprise.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): International trade leads to the development of a country's economy.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It helps in the creation of employment opportunities and increase in income.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: International trade does indeed lead to the development of a country's economy, and one of the reasons is that it helps in the creation of employment opportunities and increase in income.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): World Trade Organization (WTO) is an international organization that aims to promote free trade.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has been instrumental in reducing tariffs and other trade barriers.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The World Trade Organization (WTO) is indeed an international organization that aims to promote free trade, and one of the ways it does this is by reducing tariffs and other trade barriers.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Multinational Corporation (MNC) is a company that operates in one country.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has a limited focus on the domestic market.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: A Multinational Corporation (MNC) is a company that operates in many countries, not just one country. It has a global focus, not a limited focus on the domestic market.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Import substitution policy is a trade policy that aims to reduce imports.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It involves the promotion of domestic industries by imposing tariffs on imported goods.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The import substitution policy is indeed a trade policy that aims to reduce imports, and one of the ways it does this is by promoting domestic industries through the imposition of tariffs on imported goods.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Export Processing Zones (EPZs) are set up to cater to the requirements of the domestic market.
questions.reason: Reason (R): They are allowed to import duty-free inputs and export the final products.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Export Processing Zones (EPZs) are set up to cater to the requirements of the international market, not the domestic market. They are allowed to import duty-free inputs and export the final products.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Foreign Exchange Market (FEM) is the market where currencies are traded.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It facilitates the conversion of one currency into another.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Foreign Exchange Market (FEM) is indeed the market where currencies are traded, and one of the ways it facilitates international trade is by facilitating the conversion of one currency into another.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): International trade leads to a decrease in the standard of living of a country.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It helps in the creation of employment opportunities and increase in income.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: International trade does not lead to a decrease in the standard of living of a country. In fact, it helps in the creation of employment opportunities and increase in income, which can lead to an improvement in the standard of living.
